Sports.headline: Erling Haaland Wins Third Golden Boot To Match Harry Kane, Alan Shearer
Sports.detail: Erling Haaland has secured his third Golden Boot, putting him on par with Harry Kane and Alan Shearer.
After reaching the NBA Finals, the New York Knicks are now focused on winning the title, fulfilling owner James Dolan's initial mandate.
Sports.headline: Knicks focus on title after accomplishing Dolan's first mandate of reaching the NBA Finals
Astros throw a combined no-hitter in their 9-0 win over the Rangers.
Sports.detail: Astros starter Tatsuya Imai and two relievers combined to pitch a no-hitter in a 9-0 victory against the Rangers.
Due to an Ebola outbreak, the Congo team will need to isolate before entering the United States for the World Cup.
Sports.headline: Congo team must isolate to enter United States for World Cup amid Ebola outbreak.
USMNT roster confirmed: Zendejas in as Luna, Tessmann and Morris miss out
Sports.detail: The USMNT roster has been confirmed, with Zendejas included while Luna, Tessmann, and Morris are not on the list.

Entertainment.detail: Kylie Jenner and Timothée Chalamet were seen celebrating the New York Knicks playoff win with a kiss courtside. The couple's public display of affection occurred during the game in New York.
Entertainment.headline: Kylie Jenner and Timothée Chalamet celebrate Knicks playoff win with courtside kiss in New York
BTS wins Artist of the Year award at American Music Awards
Entertainment.detail: K-pop band BTS has won the Artist of the Year award at the 2026 American Music Awards, marking the second time they have received this award. The group also won Song of the Summer for Swim and Best Male K-Pop artist.
Drake has broken Michael Jackson's Billboard record as his song 'Janice STFU' has topped the charts. This achievement highlights Drake's continued dominance in the music industry.
Entertainment.headline: Drake breaks Michael Jackson's Billboard record as 'Janice STFU' tops charts
Luso-Chinese music arts fest aims to unite Macau, Zhuhai & Hengqin
Entertainment.detail: The Galaxy Entertainment Group presents the Luso-Chinese Music Arts Festival Macau-Zhuhai-Hengqin 2026, a cross-city cultural event featuring music, competitions, and heritage activities. The festival, running from Saturday through June 8, includes an opening concert titled “Sonic Alliance: Musical Battle” at Galaxy Macau.

Entertainment.headline: On Dragon Quest's 40th anniversary, 'it's impossible to ignore its influence'
Entertainment.detail: To commemorate the 40th anniversary of Dragon Quest, game developers are acknowledging the series' significant influence on the JRPG genre. Released on May 27, 1986, the original Dragon Quest simplified Western RPG gameplay and achieved immense popularity in Japan.

Health.detail: Researchers at Texas A&M have developed a nasal spray that appears to reverse brain aging by calming inflammation and restoring the brain's energy systems.
Health.headline: Scientists Say They've Reversed Brain Aging with a Simple Nasal Spray
Don't Blame COVID for Diabetes 'Surge' in Kids
Health.detail: The prevalence of type 2 diabetes is rising among US youth, and if we don't address the real causes, we really will see a potentially unstoppable surge.

Health.headline: Doctor evacuated from Congo feels 'helpless' watching colleagues die of Ebola
Health.detail: As Patrick LaRochelle waits in a specialized hospital room in Prague to see whether he has Ebola, he told The Post, his former colleagues in Congo are beginning to die of the disease.
Recent data provides reassurance regarding the safety of live vaccines for individuals with multiple sclerosis.
Health.headline: Reassuring Data Eases Concerns About Live Vaccines in MS

Health.detail: A newly discovered brain circuit may provide insights into how we learn from our mistakes and refine motor skills.
Health.headline: Hidden brain circuit could explain how movement errors sharpen new skills

Technology.detail: Google I/O 2026 revealed a far more aggressive AI strategy than many expected, potentially impacting the future of apps, work, and computing.
Technology.headline: Google I/O 2026 Signals an Extinction Event for Standalone Apps

Technology.detail: Meta is eliminating nearly 1,400 positions across its Seattle, Bellevue and Redmond offices, about 20% of its local workforce, as part of a companywide restructuring impacting 8,000 jobs.
Technology.headline: Meta cuts nearly 1,400 jobs in Seattle area, 20% of local workforce, in sweeping AI revamp

Technology.detail: The Computex Taipei trade show will feature keynote addresses by the CEOs of Qualcomm Inc, Intel Corp, Marvell Technology Inc and NXP Semiconductors NV, and will focus on AI and computing, robotics and mobility, and next-generation technologies.
Technology.headline: Computex to feature keynote speeches by Qualcomm, Intel, Marvell, NXP CEOs
Pope Leo XIV signs the encyclical Magnifica Humanitas at the Vatican. Pope Leo XIV: Unchecked AI Development Risks Building a New Tower of Babel
Technology.detail: Pope Leo XIV warns that unchecked AI development risks building a new Tower of Babel.

Technology.headline: AI gurus charging Wall Street banks a bomb
Technology.detail: AI trainers Felipe Sinisterra and Dave Wang are charging Wall Street banks top dollar to improve their AI plans, as big banks are looking to hire more AI specialists and shrink traditional banking roles.

Politics.detail: A federal court has blocked Alabama's plan for new congressional districts, stating that it could unfairly help Republicans. The court order could lead to a redrawing of the map before the upcoming elections.
Politics.headline: Federal court blocks new Republican-friendly voting map in Alabama
Armenia signs strategic partnership deal with US as election approaches
Politics.detail: Armenia has formalized a strategic partnership agreement with the United States as the country gears up for elections. The deal signifies closer ties between the two nations amid regional political shifts.

Politics.headline: Trump wraps up 3-hour medical visit to Walter Reed and says 'Everything checked out PERFECTLY'
Politics.detail: Donald Trump completed a three-hour medical examination at Walter Reed National Military Medical Center and declared that his health is excellent. The visit comes after increased public scrutiny of the former president's health.

The Trump administration has proposed the implementation of Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs) for all federal employees in the United States. This measure aims to prevent leaks of information, but it has raised concerns about transparency.
Politics.headline: White House proposes NDAs for all US federal workers
South Carolina Senate rejects Trump's call to redraw congressional map for midterm elections
Politics.detail: The South Carolina Senate has declined President Trump's request to redraw the state's congressional map for the midterm elections. This rejection highlights the ongoing tensions between the state legislature and the Trump administration.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: No one is an island
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Taiwanese illustrator Chuchumei was told she was not allowed to take part in the Hong Kong Illustration and Creative Show just two days before the exhibition started because she published a cartoon on social media in October.

After a 29-year-old woman employed at a horror-themed escape room in Taipei died due to a performance accident earlier this month, the Taipei City Government said that it had already required escape room operators to incorporate public safety management measures and had issued safety guidelines.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: Execution, not legislation, lacking
Editorial Board. Using D.C. pensions to finance housing is a recipe for a fiscal mess.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Washington Post Editorial Board discusses the potential problems with using D.C. pensions to finance housing.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial Board. Abdul El-Sayed's Medicare muddle.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Washington Post Editorial Board analyzes Abdul El-Sayed's stance on Medicare.

An editorial discusses the challenges faced by the Korean government as it shifts its social security policy towards universal welfare, emphasizing the difficulty in securing the necessary funding and the potential harm to vulnerable groups. It suggests establishing a productive welfare system and restructuring mandatory expenditures to ensure sustainability.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: Korea's Shift to Universal Welfare Faces Massive Funding Challenge

Science.headline: Record temps as spring heat wave bakes Europe
Science.detail: Western Europe is experiencing a record-breaking heat wave, with temperatures soaring well above normal levels for May, and scientists attribute the amplifying of such extremes to human-driven climate change. The U.K. reported its hottest-ever May day at 35.1 degrees Celsius, while France also logged its hottest-ever May day.

Science.headline: JWST Reveals A Star-Scorched Mercury-Like Planet That's Bigger Than Earth
Science.detail: The James Webb Space Telescope has observed a Mercury-like planet that is scorched by its nearby star, revealing new details about exoplanets. This discovery helps scientists understand the diversity of planets beyond our solar system.

Science.headline: 'Mammoth' Bones Kept in a Museum For 70 Years Turn Out to Be Entirely Different Animal
Science.detail: Bones initially classified as a mammoth and stored in a museum for seven decades have been identified as belonging to a different animal. This discovery highlights the importance of re-evaluating historical collections with modern scientific techniques.

Researchers have identified a ring-shaped area beyond Jupiter's orbit that acted as a versatile breeding ground for planetesimals, potentially explaining the origins of various meteorite types. Simulations suggest diverse planetesimal populations formed in this region over millions of years, connecting these objects to known meteorite groups found on Earth.
Science.headline: Scientists discover a giant “planet factory” beyond Jupiter
NGC 3660 and Burçin's Galaxy
Science.detail: The Astronomy Picture of the Day highlights two galaxies: NGC 3660, a barred spiral galaxy with active star formation, and Burçin's Galaxy, which has a central region of older stars surrounded by rings of younger material, possibly from accreting other galaxies. The image showcases the distinct characteristics of each galaxy and the processes that may have shaped them.

World.detail: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon resulted in 31 deaths, and Iran has accused the U.S. of violating the ceasefire agreement amid ongoing tensions in the region. The situation remains volatile as attacks and counter-accusations escalate.
World.headline: Israel kills 31 in Lebanon attacks; Iran accuses US of violating ceasefire

World.detail: A chemical tank rupture at Nippon Dynawave Packaging Co. in Washington state has resulted in multiple fatalities and injuries. The tank held approximately 900,000 gallons of white liquor, and investigations are underway to determine the cause of the incident.
World.headline: Chemical tank rupture kills multiple people in US state of Washington

World.detail: An intense and early heat wave is impacting Europe, leading to record-breaking temperatures and heat-related deaths in several countries. France has reported seven heat-related deaths as western Europe experiences extreme conditions for May.
World.headline: Exceptionally early heat wave shatters records and brings deaths in Europe
Russia says US did not grant visa for vice-minister to attend UN Security Council meeting
World.detail: Russia's UN ambassador stated that the U.S. did not issue a visa for Deputy Foreign Minister Alexander Alimov to attend a UN Security Council meeting. Russia considers this a breach of U.S. obligations under the UN Headquarters Agreement.

World.headline: Ebola Outbreak in the DRC Raises Global Health Concerns Amid Conflict and Displacement
World.detail: A significant increase in Ebola cases has been reported in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, particularly in Ituri Province, with additional cases in Kampala, Uganda. The outbreak is linked to insecurity and displacement, raising concerns that it could spread without effective monitoring.

Finance & Economy.detail: Oil prices fell in early Asian trade on Wednesday as negotiations between the U.S. and Iran continued despite...
Finance & Economy.headline: Oil prices fall with US-Iran negotiations, Hormuz deal in focus

Finance & Economy.detail: Elon Musk has discussed with colleagues the possibility of combining SpaceX with Tesla, according to a CNBC...
Finance & Economy.headline: Musk mulled combining Tesla and SpaceX - report

Finance & Economy.detail: SK Hynix Inc (KS:000660) on Wednesday became the second South Korean company to enter the trillion dollar club, as...
Finance & Economy.headline: SK Hynix crosses $1 trillion market cap on AI boom

Finance & Economy.detail: U.S. President Donald Trump has appointed former U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi to a White House...
Finance & Economy.headline: Trump appoints former Attorney General Bondi to White House AI panel, Axios reports
S&P, Nasdaq end at records as Micron Technology surges to $1 trillion market cap
Finance & Economy.detail: Wall Street ended largely higher on Tuesday, helped by a jump in chip stocks led by Micron Technology.

Zimbabwe.headline: Six killed in Harare-Bulawayo Road crash near Redcliff
Zimbabwe.detail: A road accident on the Harare-Bulawayo Road near Redcliff has claimed the lives of six people.

Zimbabwe.headline: Police nab suspect linked to Cross Dete–Binga Road hit-and-run accident
Zimbabwe.detail: Police have arrested a suspect in connection with a hit-and-run accident on the Cross Dete-Binga Road.
Dreamstar Zimbabwe is set to return for its 10th anniversary season, celebrating a decade of talent discovery and development.
Zimbabwe.headline: Dreamstar Zimbabwe returns for landmark 10th anniversary season
Rural transformation drive goes a gear up
Zimbabwe.detail: Efforts to transform rural areas in Zimbabwe are gaining momentum.

Zimbabwe.headline: President mourns Masarira
Zimbabwe.detail: The President of Zimbabwe has expressed his condolences following the death of Linda Masarira.

Education.headline: NEET-UG candidates can submit bank details for fee refund till May 27: NTA
Education.detail: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has extended the deadline for NEET-UG candidates to submit their bank details for fee refunds until May 27, 2026.
UK universities are warning of potential cuts affecting impoverished students due to ongoing funding challenges.
Education.headline: UK universities warn of cuts for impoverished students if dire funding issues continue.
Parliament Panel Summons Exam Bodies, CBSE Officials Over NEET Row, OSM System
Education.detail: A parliamentary panel has summoned exam bodies and CBSE officials to address concerns regarding the NEET row and the Online System Management (OSM).
Sasbadi Holdings Bhd has secured a contract from the Ministry of Education (MoE) to publish, print and supply a textbook package under the new School Curriculum 2027.
Education.headline: Sasbadi secures MoE textbook contract under School Curriculum 2027

Education.detail: The U.S. Department of Education and the Department of Labor have announced additional competitions aimed at promoting civics education.
Education.headline: ED & DOL Announce Additional Competitions to Promote Civics

Crime & Justice.detail: Public defenders are requesting the Tennessee governor to stop executions following a failed execution attempt. The state was unable to execute an inmate.
Crime & Justice.headline: Public defenders ask Tennessee governor to halt executions after state failed to execute inmate
Wander Franco criminally responsible for abuse, judge finds, but spared punishment in Dominican case
Crime & Justice.detail: A Dominican judge has found Wander Franco criminally responsible for the sexual and psychological abuse of a minor but will not serve a sentence.

The convictions of a young teenage girl, Ms AB, have been sent back to the courts by the Criminal Cases Review Commission (CCRC), the first such referral since the publication of Baroness Louise Casey's report on institutional failures to protect children from grooming gangs.
Crime & Justice.headline: First grooming gang case sent back to the courts by CCRC following Casey Report

Crime & Justice.detail: The Southern Poverty Law Center asked a federal judge to dismiss the criminal charges filed against it by the Justice Department, saying the indictment represents a "top-down, retributive campaign" directed by President Trump.
Crime & Justice.headline: SPLC seeks dismissal of criminal charges, saying prosecution is vindictive
Former Bangladesh PM sentenced to death for crimes against humanity.
Crime & Justice.detail: The former Prime Minister of Bangladesh has been sentenced to death for crimes against humanity.

Lifestyle.detail: Randeep Hooda expresses his joy and affection after welcoming a baby with Lin Laishram, describing himself as being 'in teenage love'.
Lifestyle.headline: Why Randeep Hooda Is 'In Teenage Love' After Welcoming Baby With Lin Laishram
Women reveal surprising effects of being bald: 'It's a luxury'
Lifestyle.detail: Women are sharing the unexpected benefits and freedoms they've experienced after choosing to shave their heads.
A sex educator explains their reasoning for not defining sex, offering a unique perspective on the topic.
Lifestyle.headline: What does sex mean to you? I'm a sex educator – here's why I don't define it at all

Lifestyle.detail: For those who prefer a more luxurious outdoor experience, Malaysia offers a variety of glamping destinations.
Lifestyle.headline: Don't like to camp? Don't worry, Malaysia has cool glamping spots too.
Buffy The Vampire Slayer star Charisma Carpenter, 55, showcases ageless physique in sun-soaked bikini photos.
Lifestyle.detail: Charisma Carpenter, at 55 years old, displays her stunning figure in bikini photos shared on social media.
